text:
Market today: Risk-off mood. Inflation topping 4% with Fed pressure easing sounds like stagflation-lite — bad for rate-sensitive growth and tech multiples. Grantham calling this the priciest market ever adds sentiment weight. Micron weakness drags chip sentiment, which hits your whole book.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 16% and flagged SELL as your largest position; on margin that pain is real and compounding. PLTR is down nearly 14% and also flagged SELL — two simultaneous exits is a lot to manage, prioritize AVGO first. SPAL is down 17% — it's a 2x leveraged ETF with daily decay; every down day costs you twice, and your stop at $19 is close.

Watch: 1) AVGO price action around $336 stop — breach it and you cut, no debate. 2) Broader chip sentiment via SMH; if your only green chip name starts rolling over, the whole book gets uglier fast.

Bottom line: You have two SELL-flagged positions bleeding on margin — AVGO and PLTR need a decision today, not tomorrow.
